body.title: Acquisition channel registry — Channel abstraction with constraints
body.result: Channel abstraction and registry implemented with 5 acquisition channels (FileInbox, DirectURL, Calibre, GeminiSearch, BrowserUse) in priority order per D230/D231. Registry pattern supports constraints, cost models, retry policies, short-circuits. All 17 tests passing.
body.description:
D230. Define Channel abstraction and registry.

Each channel entry has:
- name: str
- acquire(query: AcquireRequest) -> AcquireResult | ChannelFailure
- constraints: list[str]
- cost_model: {per_call_usd, reliability: high/medium/low}
- one_way: bool
- retry_policy: {max_attempts, backoff_sec}
- short_circuits: bool  # if True, a hit on this channel skips remaining channels

Initial channels to register:
1. CalibreChannel — local EPUB via open_book/read_chunk; free; high reliability; one_way=True
2. FileInboxChannel — ~/.TheIgors/igor_wild_0001/inbox/ dropped files; free; high reliability; short_circuits=True
3. DirectURLChannel — explicit URL or file path provided by caller; free; fetches directly; short_circuits=True (a direct URL/path prevents any further search)
4. GeminiSearchChannel — uses Igor browser profile (free Gemini) for web search + synthesis; medium reliability; used when "dig more" research requested
5. BrowserUseChannel — browser-use agent for structured acquisition (Kindle, paywalled); constraints: NO account creation, NO book switching, NO login prompts (fail cleanly); last resort

File: wild_igor/igor/network/channels/ (new directory)
Inertia: LOW
Size: M
